title: Rolling Kontoyiannis entropy of encoded returns (efficiency regime, AFML Ch18)
source: LdP literature mining (2 parallel read-only agents over AFML Ch5/17/18/19 + HFT, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/extracted), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'hay alguna otra nueva feature que se puede extraer de la literatura'.
why: Entropy of the encoded return sequence measures market EFFICIENCY/predictability — a regime descriptor absent today and orthogonal to rv_regime (a low-vol regime can be high- or low-entropy). Clean meta-labeling hook (AFML §18.1): 'momentum bets are more profitable when prices carry little information; mean-reversion when prices carry a lot.' Kontoyiannis/LZ estimator (Snippet 18.4) is the efficient choice. Synergy: encode the FFD series (B0134) not raw returns, per §18.5.
value_rationale: WORTH-TESTING. Genuinely orthogonal efficiency-regime feature; gated by short-window bias on D1 and encoding/window causality traps.
scope: Implement konto (Snippet 18.4) over a trailing window (100-256 bars); use QUANTILE or SIGMA encoding (>=8-10 letters), NOT binary (heteroscedastic |ret| on daily time bars)
Quantile boundaries fit on TRAIN slice only (lookahead trap); trailing window, not centered/full-sample
Encode the FFD(d) series from B0134 rather than raw returns (§18.5 advice) — retains memory
Accept moderate O(n^2)-per-window compute; budget for it
recommendation: Test after B0134 (uses its output as the encoded series). Honest flag: D1 windows sit at the short end of estimator reliability (no volume clock).
out_of_scope: Plug-in entropy (Snippet 18.1 — noisier dup on short windows, SKIP). Entropy-implied vol (collinear with rv_20). PIN/VPIN/portfolio entropy (need cross-section or tick data).

history.reason: LdP literature mining: Kontoyiannis entropy = orthogonal efficiency-regime feature; encode FFD series
